name: Economic Order Quantity (EOQ)
description: The order quantity that minimises total ordering plus holding cost, balancing the fixed cost of ordering against the cost of carrying inventory.

inputs.name: Carrying cost rate
inputs.description: Annual holding cost as a fraction of unit cost, e.g. 0.21 for 21%.

assumptions: Ordering cost and unit cost are constant (no quantity discounts).
The full order arrives at once and stock is drawn down linearly.
limitations: Not appropriate for just-in-time items or highly variable demand.
Ignores quantity discounts, capacity limits, and order-investment ceilings.
Result is typically rounded to a practical order/pack multiple.

citations.quote: EOQ = sqrt((2 x Demand(D) x Ordering cost(OC)) / (Carrying cost %(k) x Unit cost(UC)))
The formula for EOQ is Q = the square root 2 x D O / P H where D is annual demand, O is the cost of raising an order, P is the price per unit and H is the holding cost.
